commands_and_summary:
Call to tool write_to_file with arguments {"filename": "Dockerfile", "text": "FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git curl wget && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/latest/download/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p /opt/conda \
    && rm /tmp/miniforge.sh

ENV PATH="/opt/conda/bin:$PATH"

WORKDIR /app

RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git .

RUN wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iris-sast/iris/main/environment.yml

RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

CMD ["/bin/bash"]"}

commands_and_summary.summary: The Docker build failed for the image 'tooliris_image:executionagent-1777588818-d80904dd' due to an error in the RUN command, which attempted to install packages including git, curl, and wget, and failed to execute the Miniforge installation script.
commands_and_summary.Setup details::
Relevant dependencies: The installation requires `git`, `curl`, `wget`, and the Miniforge script located at 'https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/latest/download/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh'. 
Important commands: The command that failed is: `apt-get update && apt-get install -y git curl wget && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* && wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/latest/download/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh && chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh && /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p /opt/conda && rm /tmp/miniforge.sh`. 
Important files: The Dockerfile at path '/Users/alisonteske/cs/thesis/execution-agent-test/ExecutionAgent/execution_agent_workspace/execution_agent/iris/Dockerfile_path' should be checked for potential syntax issues or missing configurations.
commands_and_summary.Meaningful next steps:
1. Review the complete build log to identify specific errors that caused the failure, particularly lines starting with 'ERROR', 'failed', 'cannot', or 'E:'. This is crucial for understanding what went wrong in the build process. 
2. Ensure that all network connectivity is functioning correctly since this may prevent necessary packages from downloading. Additionally, verify that the package names in the Dockerfile are correct for the Ubuntu 22.04 environment. 
3. Try building again after addressing the identified issues, as resolving these may allow for successful completion of the Docker image build.

commands_and_summary:
Call to tool write_to_file with arguments {"filename": "Dockerfile", "text": "FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git curl wget && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/latest/download/Miniforge3-Linux-aarch64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p /opt/conda \
    && rm /tmp/miniforge.sh

ENV PATH="/opt/conda/bin:$PATH"

WORKDIR /app

RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git .

RUN wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iris-sast/iris/main/environment.yml

RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

CMD ["/bin/bash"]"}

written_files:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git curl wget && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/latest/download/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p /opt/conda \
    && rm /tmp/miniforge.sh

ENV PATH="/opt/conda/bin:$PATH"

WORKDIR /app

RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git .

RUN wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iris-sast/iris/main/environment.yml

RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git curl wget && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/latest/download/Miniforge3-Linux-aarch64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p /opt/conda \
    && rm /tmp/miniforge.sh

ENV PATH="/opt/conda/bin:$PATH"

WORKDIR /app

RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git .

RUN wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iris-sast/iris/main/environment.yml

RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

previous_attempts.problems: Multiple failures occurred during the Docker image build process, primarily related to the installation of Miniconda and Miniforge. The specific error messages included: 'failed ELF opening leading to a non-zero exit code' during Miniconda installation, and 'unknown query {CWE}' when running the tests. The Docker build often failed at steps involving the installation of these dependencies, indicating potential compatibility or configuration issues. Additionally, there were issues with missing files, specifically 'src/iris.py', which caused subsequent attempts to run the script to fail.
previous_attempts.actions: 1. Initiated the Docker build with a Dockerfile based on Ubuntu 22.04 and added commands to install dependencies. 2. Attempted to install Miniconda but faced ELF file errors and build failures. 3. Switched to Miniforge as an alternative, which also resulted in installation failures. 4. Modified the Dockerfile to include commands to accept terms of service for Conda channels before installation. 5. Successfully set up the Conda environment with the provided 'environment.yml', but subsequent queries against the script failed due to missing parameters and files. 6. Attempted to run tests which failed due to unknown queries.
previous_attempts.progress: 50% complete. The Conda environment was created successfully, and the Docker image was built after several iterations. However, the project is still not fully functional due to missing files and parameters required for testing. The Docker container is operational but does not currently execute tests successfully.
previous_attempts.lessons: The project requires specific versions of Miniconda or Miniforge for compatibility. The installation of dependencies needs careful handling of environment variables and terms of service acceptance. The script 'src/iris.py' requires specific input parameters which were not provided. Compatibility issues with ELF files suggest a need to verify the architecture compatibility of the dependencies being installed.
previous_attempts.suggestions: 1. Verify the architecture compatibility of the Miniconda or Miniforge installer being used. 2. Ensure the 'src/iris.py' script exists and is accessible before executing it. 3. When running tests, provide valid query parameters instead of placeholders like '{CWE}'. 4. Consider using a stable version of Miniforge known to work with Ubuntu 22.04. 5. Review the README.md for any specific requirements or configurations not yet addressed. 6. If the current approach continues to fail, consider using a pre-built Docker image known to work for this project.
previous_attempts.dockerfile_recommendation:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git curl wget
RUN apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends tzdata bash ca-certificates python3 python3-pip

WORKDIR /app

RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git
WORKDIR /app/iris

ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH

RUN wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/download/4.10.3-10/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
    rm /tmp/miniforge.sh && \
    conda clean -afy

RUN conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main \
    && conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r

COPY environment.yml /app/iris/
RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

CMD ["/bin/bash"]
previous_attempts.dockerfile_used:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    curl sudo nano software-properties-common vim wget python3 python3-pip unzip tar apt-transport-https lsb-release \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git

WORKDIR /app/iris

ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH

RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-aarch64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    chmod +x /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
    rm /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
    conda clean -afy

RUN conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main \
    && conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r

RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
RUN echo "conda activate iris" >> ~/.bashrc

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

previous_attempts.problems: The Docker build process encountered multiple failures related to the installation of Miniconda and Miniforge, particularly during commands that attempted to download and execute the installer scripts. The failures were marked by errors such as 'failed to open ELF file' and missing commands related to conda during environment setup steps. These issues appeared consistently across various attempts, indicating potential version incompatibilities or network issues in accessing the required installation files. The build was initiated using both Python 3.10-slim and Ubuntu 22.04 as base images, yet neither approach resolved the installation failures.
previous_attempts.actions: 1. The initial Dockerfile used Ubuntu 22.04 and attempted to install Miniforge, which failed at multiple stages due to issues with the ELF file. 2. Changed to Python 3.10-slim as a base image, expecting better compatibility with the project dependencies. 3. Attempted to install Miniconda subsequently, which also faced failures at the installation stage. 4. Repeated attempts were made to download the environment.yml file and create the conda environment, but all efforts were met with similar installation issues. 5. The last successful step was the building of a Docker image, but subsequent attempts to execute conda commands failed due to the missing installation of conda.
previous_attempts.progress: The Docker image was built successfully, and essential packages like git, curl, and wget were installed. However, the attempts to install Miniforge or Miniconda consistently failed, preventing the creation of the conda environment from the environment.yml file. Therefore, the environment setup is incomplete, and overall progress is approximately 40%. The project dependencies are not yet fully installed, and no tests have been executed.
previous_attempts.lessons: The project requires Miniforge or Miniconda for managing Python environments, specifically with Python 3.10 as a dependency. The environment.yml file specifies dependencies that include libraries like Pandas, Requests, and PyTorch, among others. It was observed that network issues or incorrect file paths led to failures during the installation of Miniconda and Miniforge. Additionally, ensuring that the base image is compatible with the architecture and available package versions is crucial.
previous_attempts.suggestions: 1. Ensure a stable internet connection during the Docker build process to avoid download failures. 2. Use a stable version of Miniconda or Miniforge that is confirmed to work with Ubuntu 22.04; consider using an official version from Anaconda's repository. 3. Modify the Dockerfile to include explicit error handling for the installation commands. 4. Validate the environment.yml file to ensure all listed dependencies are compatible with Python 3.10. 5. If the problems persist, consider using a different base image like Python 3.10 full version instead of slim to provide more compatibility.

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Update and install dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git curl wget \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && wget --quiet https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/latest/download/Miniforge3-Linux-aarch64.sh -O /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && chmod +x /tmp/miniforge.sh \
    && /tmp/miniforge.sh -b -p /opt/conda \
    && rm /tmp/miniforge.sh

# Ensure conda is in PATH
ENV PATH="/opt/conda/bin:$PATH"

# Clone the repository
RUN git clone https://github.com/iris-sast/iris.git /app

# Access the cloned directory
WORKDIR /app

# Download the environment.yml file
RUN wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iris-sast/iris/main/environment.yml

# Create the conda environment
RUN conda env create -f environment.yml

# Set command for the container
CMD ["/bin/bash"]
